briefdescription.para: < eFuse secure control register offsets and definitions
This function check whether hash calculation is completed or not.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.

listitem.para: Check for SHA operation is completed with in Timeout(10sec) or not

briefdescription.para: This function performs NIST padding for different SHA algorithms according to their respective specifications and transfers the combined partial data + padding to the SHA engine via DMA.

para.verbatim: Any data buffered in PartialData from XSecure_ShaDmaXfer is kept in place and the NIST padding is appended directly after it. The combined buffer is then DMA'd as a single transfer.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - Upon invalid SHA algorithm parameter.

simplesect.para: This function applies algorithm-specific NIST padding: SHA2 - 0x80 start byte + zero padding + message length in bits (big-endian) SHA3 - 0x06 start byte + zero padding + 0x80 end byte SHAKE - 0x1F start byte + zero padding + 0x80 end byte

listitem.para: Determine LengthFieldSize based on hash algorithm
Zeroize the padding area in PartialData after the buffered data
Reset PartialLen before sending, since the combined data is already assembled in PartialData and XSecure_ShaDmaXfer manages PartialLen internally on platforms that require staging.
Send combined partial data + NIST padding to SHA engine

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 DataAddr, u32 Len, u32 IsLastUpdate)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDmaTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function transfers data to the SHA engine using DMA and manages the complete transfer process including SSS configuration.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.
- Address of the data to be transferred to SHA engine.
- Length of the data to be transferred in bytes.
- Flag indicating if this is the last data transfer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - Upon successful data transfer completion.
XST_FAILURE - Upon any failure during the transfer process.
XSECURE_SHA_DMA_TRANSFER_ERROR - Upon any failure during the transfer process.
Configure the SSS for SHA2/3 hashing.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XSecure_ShaConfig structure based on the unique device id.

parameterdescription.para: Unique device ID of the device for the lookup operation.

listitem.para: It returns CfgPtr which is a reference to a config record in the configuration table
It returns NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes SHA instance so that it is ready to be used.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.
- Pointer to the DMA instance.

listitem.para: Initializes the secure stream switch instance

briefdescription.para: This function validates the SHA Mode (refer XSecure_ShaMode enum) and configures SSS and start the SHA engine.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.
- Indicates SHA3/SHA2 shall be operated in which sha mode that is SHA-384/256/512

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - Invalid parameter (InstancePtr is NULL or invalid SHA mode)
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - SHA engine is not in initialized state
Validate SHA state is initialized or not
Validate the SHA mode and initialize SHA instance based on SHA mode.
Select SHA Mode based on SHA type.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the block length for the given SHA algorithm.

parameterdescription.para: SHA algorithm mode.

simplesect.para: Block length in bytes, or 0 for invalid algorithm.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 DataAddr, const u32 DataSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function updates input data to SHA Engine to calculate Hash.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the SHA instance.
Pointer to the input data for hashing.
Size of the input data in bytes.

listitem.para: Validate SHA state is started or not.
If the input data is of 0 length, do not initiate DMA transfer
Transfer input data to SHA engine using DMA
Set UPDATE_DONE only when IsLastUpdate is TRUE and all data has been DMA'd (PartialLen == 0). If partial data remains, SW NIST padding in ShaFinish will handle the final transfer.
Set SHA2/3 under reset on failure condition

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, u64 HashAddr, u32 HashBufSize, u32 ReqHashSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ExtendedShaFinish

briefdescription.para: This function calculates and reads the final hash of input data with support for variable-length hash output.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the SHA instance.
Pointer to the buffer where the final hash will be stored.
Size allocated for the hash buffer in bytes. Must be at least as large as ReqHashSize to avoid buffer overflow.
Requested hash size in bytes to be read from the SHA digest. Must not exceed XSECURE_MAX_HASH_SIZE_IN_BYTES.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - Upon successful hash calculation and retrieval.
XST_FAILURE - Upon failure during hash completion or digest read.
XSECURE_SHA_INVALID_PARAM - When InstancePtr is NULL or HashBufSize is less than ReqHashSize.
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - When SHA engine is in initialized state without any update.
XSECURE_SHA_NIST_PADDING_ERROR - When NIST padding operation fails.
Validate Hash buffer size to avoid buffer overflow.

inbodydescription.para:
Switch padding to SW based padding to address the following scenarios.
SHA zero-data length use case.
SHA Final call without preceding SHA update call with IsLast set to TRUE use case.
Perform NIST padding and send to SHA engine
Check the SHA2/3 DONE bit.
Read the Hash (word-aligned) and store in Hash Buffer.
Handle last partial word if present
Set SHA2/3 under reset.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates and reads the final hash of input data.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the SHA instance.
- Pointer which holds final hash.
- Size allocated for Hash Buffer

listitem.para: Validate Hash buffer size to avoid buffer overflow.

inbodydescription.para:
Switch padding to SW based padding to address the following scenarios.
SHA zero-data length use case.
SHA Final call without preceding SHA update call with IsLast set to TRUE use case.
Perform NIST padding and send to SHA engine
Check the SHA2/3 DONE bit.
Read out the Hash and store in Hash Buffer.
Set SHA2/3 under reset.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *const InstancePtr, XSecure_ShaMode ShaMode, u64 DataAddr, u32 DataSize, u64 HashAddr, u32 HashBufSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDigest

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the SHA2/3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the SHA instance.
Indicates SHA3/SHA2 shall be operated in which sha mode that is SHA-384/256/512
Pointer to the input data for hashing.
Size of the input data in bytes.
Pointer which holds final hash.
Size allocated for Hash Buffer

listitem.para: Validate the SHA mode and initialize SHA instance based on SHA mode.
Configure SSS and start the SHA engine.
Update input data to SHA Engine to calculate Hash.
Calculate and read the final hash of input data.

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Sha instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 SubsystemId, XSecure_Sha *XSecureShaInstPtr, u32 AddrLow, u32 AddrHigh)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaOperation

briefdescription.para: This function performs SHA operation based on operation flags provided in IPI payload and returns the result.

parameterdescription.para: Subsystem ID.
Pointer to the SHA instance
Lower 32 bit address of XSecure_ShaOpParams structure
upper 32 bit address of XSecure_ShaOpParams structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the SHA operation is successful
XSECURE_ERR_INVALID_ADDR_RANGE - If address range validation fails
Validate internal address fields in the copied structure

briefdescription.para: This function calls respective IPI handler based on the API_ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the handler execution is successful
XST_INVALID_PARAM - If Cmd is NULL or invalid API ID

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XSecure_ImageInfo structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on successful verification.
Error code on failure.

listitem.para: If PUF helper data exists in boot header
Copy PPK to global variable for future use

briefdescription.para: This function decrypts the image partition.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XSecure_ImageInfo structure
Pointer to single partition non-bitstream image
Pointer to User Key
Location of the secure image loaded after validation

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on successful verification.
Error code on failure.

listitem.para: Linux or U-boot stores Key in the form of String So this conversion is required here.
XilSecure expects Key in big endian form
Initialize the AES driver so that it's ready to use

briefdescription.para: This function performs authentication of a given data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to auhentication parameter structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
XST_FAILURE on failure

listitem.para: Calculate Hash on Data to be authenticated

briefdescription.para: This function is used to initialize the DMA driver.

simplesect.para: returns the error code on any error returns XST_SUCCESS on success

briefdescription.para: This function is used to initialize CsuDma and return CsuDma pointer.

simplesect.para: returns reference to a XCsuDma driver instance

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 SrcAddrHigh, u32 SrcAddrLow, u32 SrcSize, u32 Flags)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3Hash

briefdescription.para: This function access the xilsecure SHA3 hardware based on the flags provided to calculate the SHA3 hash.

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32-bit of the input or output address.
Lower 32-bit of the input or output address.
Size of the data on which hash should be calculated.

inputs for the operation requested BIT(0) - for initializing csudma driver and SHA3, (Here address and size inputs can be NULL) BIT(1) - To call Sha3_Update API which can be called multiple times when data is not contiguous. BIT(2) - to get final hash of the whole updated data. Hash will be overwritten at provided address with 48 bytes.

simplesect.para: Returns Status XST_SUCCESS on success and error code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 SrcAddrHigh, u32 SrcAddrLow, u32 SrcSize, u32 Flags)
memberdef.name: XSecure_RsaCore

briefdescription.para: This is the function to RSA decrypt or encrypt the provided data and load back to memory.

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32-bit Linear memory space from where CSUDMA will read the data
Lower 32-bit Linear memory space from where CSUDMA will read the data
Number of bytes to be encrypted or decrypted.
Flags for operation, where BIT(0) indicates Encryption/Decryption (0 for RSA Private key Decryption or 1 for RSA Public key Encryption)

simplesect.para: Returns Status XST_SUCCESS on success and error code on failure.

Data to be encrypted/Decrypted + Modulus + Exponent Modulus and Data should always be key size Exponent : private key's exponent is key size while decrypting the signature and 32 bit for public key for encrypting the signature In this API we are not taking exponentiation value.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the AES-GCM engine with Key and Iv.

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.
Lower 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

listitem.para: Initialize the Aes driver so that it's ready to use

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.
Lower 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

briefdescription.para: This function checks for flags field of the XSecure_AesParams struct and resolves weather the request is encryption or decryption.

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.
Lower 32 bit address of the XSecure_AesParams structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

briefdescription.para: This function performs authentication of data by encrypting the signature with provided key and compares with hash of the data and returns success or failure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the RSA signature of the data
Pointer to XSecure_RsaKey structure.
Pointer to the hash of the data to be authenticated.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

listitem.para: Compare encrypted signature with sha3 hash calculated on data

briefdescription.para: This function performs authentication of a partition of an image.

Pointer to partition to be authenticated.

Pointer to authentication certificate of the partition.

Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

pointer to boot header of the image.
Size of the boot header.
Pointer to authentication certificate.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
XST_FAILURE on failure

briefdescription.para: This function copies the data from specified source to destination using CSU DMA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the destination address.
Pointer to the source address.
Data size to be copied.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
XST_FAILURE on failure

listitem.para: Data transfer in loop back mode
Polling for transfer to be done
To acknowledge the transfer has completed

briefdescription.para: This function verifies SPK by authenticating SPK with PPK, also checks either SPK is revoked or not.if it is not boot header authentication.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the authentication certificate.
Input variable which holds efuse RSA authentication or boot header authentication (0 for Boot header authentication or 1 for RSA authentication)

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

listitem.para: Check the cache value with the value passed
Verify SPK with PPK in authentication certificate

briefdescription.para: This function authenticates the single partition image's boot header and image header, also copies all the required details to the ImageInfo pointer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the single partition image.
Pointer to XSecure_ImageInfo structure.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

listitem.para: XSECURE_AUTH_NOT_ENABLED - represents image is not authenticated.

simplesect.para: Copies the header and authentication certificate to internal buffer.

listitem.para: Copy boot header to internal buffer
Copy Image header authentication certificate to local memory
Copy image header to internal memory
Partition header offset is w.r.t to image start address
Add partition header IV to boot header IV

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 AddrHigh, u32 AddrLow, u32 KupAddrHigh, u32 KupAddrLow, XSecure_DataAddr *Addr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SecureImage

briefdescription.para: This function process the secure image of single partition created by bootgen.

parameterdescription.para: Higher 32-bit linear memory space of single partition non-bitstream image.
Lower 32-bit linear memory space of single partition non-bitstream image.
Higher 32-bit linear memory space of KUP key.
lower 32-bit linear memory space of KUP key.
Location of the secure image loaded after validation.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on success
Error code on failure

listitem.para: Error other than XSECURE_AUTH_NOT_ENABLED error will be an error
Here Buffer still contains Boot header
If authentication is enabled and authentication of partition is enabled
Copy authentication certificate to internal memory
Decrypt the partition if encryption is enabled
Add partition header IV to boot header IV

briefdescription.para: This function tells whether RSA authentication is enabled or not.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XSECURE_ENABLED if RSA bit of efuse is programmed
XSECURE_NOTENABLED if RSA bit of efuse is not programmed.

briefdescription.para: This function tells whether encrypt only is enabled or not.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XSECURE_ENABLED if enc_only bit of efuse is programmed
XSECURE_NOTENABLED if enc_only bit of efuse is not programmed

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the PPK hash with PPK programmed on efuse.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to CSU DMA instance.
Pointer to authentication certificate.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on successful verification.
Error code on failure.

Pointer to authentication certificate.

Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on successful verification.
Error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether SPK is been revoked or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to authentication certificate.

simplesect.para:
Returns Status
XST_SUCCESS on successful verification.
Error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the SecureAesExample.

detaileddescription.para: This example illustrates the usage of RSA APIs to encrypt or decrypt the signature.
Main function to call the SecureAesExample function.
Main function to call the SecureShaExample.
Main function to call the SecureRsaExample.

simplesect.para: None

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the boot image authentication Failed .

simplesect.para: None

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the SHA calculation failed.

simplesect.para: None

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS if example runs successfully
ErrorCode if the example fails.
XST_SUCCESS if the boot image authentication is successful.
ErrorCode if the boot image authentication Failed.
XST_FAILURE if the SHA calculation failed.

briefdescription.para: This function authenticates boot image located in DDR using RSA-4096 algorithm.

detaileddescription.para: The decrypted hash is matched against the calculated boot image hash. The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XSecure_Rsa driver.

simplesect.para: None.//! [RSA example]

briefdescription.para: One byte shift value for an integer.
Mask to extract least significant byte from integer values.
Start index for big-endian byte processing.
For invalid SHA operation flag checks.
Operation flags for all valid SHA operations.
Flag indicates both RSA and AES.
Flag indicates both RSA and AES.
Only boot header authentication is allowed.
No header authentication only partition authentication.
Out of range user eFuse error.
Attribute of authentication enabled partition header.
Attribute of encryption enabled partition header.
PPK select mask of authentication header.
PPK select shift of authentication header.
SPKID eFUSE select mask of authentication header.
SPKID eFUSE select shift of authentication header.
Offset of SPK signature authentication certificate.
Offset of authentication certificate boot header signature.
Offset of authentication certificate partition signature.
When boot header contains PUF helper data.

memberdef.name: XSECURE_AUTH_CERT_MIN_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: (XSECURE_AUTH_HEADER_SIZE \ + XSECURE_AUTH_CERT_USER_DATA \ + XSECURE_PPK_SIZE \ + XSECURE_PPK_SIZE \ + XSECURE_SPK_SIG_SIZE \ + XSECURE_BHDR_SIG_SIZE \ + XSECURE_FSBL_SIG_SIZE)

briefdescription.para: This example illustrates AES usage for decryption of a encrypted partition created by bootgen.

detaileddescription.para: This example requires downloading an encrypted single partition image to 0x04000000 in DDR memory, decrypted data will be updated at the same location, one can modify this addresses by changing ImageOffset and DstinationAddr variables. Example bif is: //arch = zynqmp; split = false; format = BIN the_ROM_image: { [aeskeyfile]aes.nky [keysrc_encryption]kup_key [encryption = aes]data.bin }
Following key and IV should be provided in .nky file while creating the boot image: Key 0 f878b838d8589818e868a828c8488808f070b030d0509010e060a020c0408000; IV D2450E07EA5DE0426C0FA133;

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ba 01/13/14 First Release 2.0 vns 01/17/17 For CR-964195 added required .nky fields in the comments, also print for decryption failure. 2.2 vns 07/06/16 Added doxygen tags 3.0 vns 02/27/18 Modified example to decrypt the single partition image instead of FSBL partition in boot image. 3.1 vns 04/04/18 For CR-998923, if data is greater than 1MB size, decrypted data is overwriting the part of source and decryption of image is failing, to fix this modified destination address, from now on image is overwritten with decrypted data. 4.3 har 10/12/20 Addressed security review comments

This example requires downloading an RSA authenticated(SHA-3) boot image to a location in DDR memory. MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bameta 11/04/14 First Release 2.2 vns 07/06/16 Added doxygen tags 3.0 vns 01/23/18 Added SHA3 keccak padding selection as this example illustrates FSBL partition authentication 4.0 vns 03/12/19 Modified function call XSecure_RsaDecrypt to XSecure_RsaPublicEncrypt, as XSecure_RsaDecrypt is deprecated. vns 03/26/19 Fixed compilation errors on IAR 4.3 har 10/12/20 Addressed security review comments
